what_it_does: Defines an SVG sitemap icon made from four rounded rectangles connected by a branched line structure.
observations: Uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with no fill and currentColor stroke.
Sets stroke width to 1.5 with rounded line caps and joins.
Draws one top rectangle and three lower rectangles as hierarchy nodes.
Uses one path to connect the top node to the lower row through vertical and horizontal segments.
standouts: Color inherits from surrounding text or icon context through currentColor.
The image is built only from SVG primitives: rect and path.
The geometry clearly represents a parent node branching to three child nodes.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon conventions: no fill, rounded caps, rounded joins, compact 24px coordinate system.
Uses inline SVG attributes rather than CSS classes or external styling.
Keeps the asset self-contained in a single svg element.
notes: This is a reusable outline sitemap icon asset for hierarchy or information-architecture actions.
questions: Should this icon include accessibility metadata such as title, role, or aria-hidden at the usage site?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a sitemap icon, and the SVG content draws a sitemap-style hierarchy icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a lightweight, theme-color-aware sitemap symbol for a design system icon set.
contribution: Contributes a scalable currentColor sitemap icon with rounded-outline hierarchy geometry for structural navigation or information-architecture UI contexts.
summary_for_embedding: A self-contained SVG outline sitemap icon using four rounded rectangles and connector paths within a 24 by 24 viewBox.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for supplying a reusable design-system icon that visually communicates sitemap, hierarchy, or structured navigation.
